summary: Expanded table-driven function signature typing, mostly converting remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS callbacks in game-mode, item, and toy headers into concrete prototypes. The tooling change enhanced tools/table-typer so it can infer more table callback types, parse broader declaration forms, and optionally operate conservatively by only replacing unknown signatures. The PR body says this reduced tables with unknown functions to a small list of obscure symbols.
what_changed: Added CFuncType.isUnk() to identify signatures that still use UNK_RET or a sole UNK_PARAMS parameter.
Added a conservative mode to the table-typer fixtab command: -conservative only updates signatures that are still unknown.
Expanded table-typer struct type knowledge with MajorScene and MinorSceneHandler callback layouts.
Improved parseTableDecls to match multi-line table declarations, comma-separated declarations, array declarators, and declarations ending in either ';' or '='.
Hardened parseCFuncValue by bailing on signatures it cannot parse, with a TODO for multi-line signatures.
Improved parameter parsing for two-token type prefixes such as 'struct' and 'unsigned'.

Changed multiple game-mode callbacks from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void(void) signatures, including gm_801737E8_OnLoad, gm_80186DFC_OnFrame, gm_8018776C_OnFrame, gm_8018838C_OnFrame, gm_8019B2DC_OnFrame, gm_801AF568_OnFrame, gm_801B6834, and gm_801B685C.
smart_moves: The conservative flag prevents table-typer from overwriting already-known or manually refined prototypes, making batch signature updates safer for decomp headers.
Adding explicit MajorScene and MinorSceneHandler layouts let the tool derive callback signatures from known table structure instead of relying on one-off manual edits.
The parser improvements target real C declaration patterns found in this codebase: multi-line table declarations, arrays, comma-separated declarations, pointer/array decorations, and two-word types.
Keeping unhandled multi-line function signatures as a safe parse failure avoids corrupting prototypes while documenting the remaining parser limitation.
The PR applied tooling changes and concrete header updates together, demonstrating that the table-typer enhancements immediately reduced unknown callback prototypes.
decomp_lessons: When a table struct is understood, encode its callback field types in tooling so many unknown function prototypes can be fixed consistently.
For broad header cleanup, add guardrails that only replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS unless an intentional full rewrite is requested.
Table declaration parsers need to handle C syntax beyond the simplest 'Type name' form, especially arrays and comma-separated globals.
Do not force parsing of signatures the tool cannot safely understand; skipping unsupported forms is preferable to generating incorrect declarations.
Callbacks named OnFrame, OnLoad, OnInit, OnLeave, and similar table-derived names often have predictable signatures once their scene or item table layout is typed.
naming_conventions: Scene lifecycle callback suffixes are kept in symbol names, such as _OnLoad, _OnFrame, _OnEnter, _OnLeave, and _OnInit.
Address-based placeholder names remain when no semantic name is known, for example gm_801B6834, gm_801B685C, and un_803122D0_OnInit.
Item callback names use the item prefix and motion/callback role where known, as in itSeakchain_UnkMotion4_Anim.
Table-typer struct field callback names include Load, Unload, Init, OnFrame, OnLoad, OnLeave, and unk_func.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was shown in the excerpt; the work is primarily signature propagation from typed C table structures.
The practical matching tactic is to type function pointer table fields, then use a tool to update matching function prototypes across headers and C files.
Use conservative signature fixing when table-derived types are likely correct but existing non-unknown prototypes should not be disturbed.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.

The only human text excerpt from the PR body states that this reduced the list of tables with unknown functions to a handful of obscure symbols, including lb_803BA248, lbl_803B7B68, lbl_803BCA24, lbl_803D6300, lbl_803D9FD8, and multiple un_803Fxxxx tables.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that handle table-typer TODO for multi-line signatures.
Search for remaining PRs that type the obscure tables listed in the PR body: lb_803BA248, lbl_803B7B68, lbl_803BCA24, lbl_803D6300, lbl_803D9FD8, and un_803Fxxxx.
Look for subsequent uses of table-typer -conservative in bulk signature cleanup.
Check later changes to MajorScene or MinorSceneHandler struct definitions to confirm or refine callback field types.
